name: Digestion and Absorption (FA ONLY)
full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Digestion and Absorption (FA ONLY)

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Digestion and Absorption (FA ONLY)

questions.question: The teeth occur in human beings are:
questions.question_raw: The teeth occur in human beings are:
questions.explanation: Each tooth is embedded in a socket of a jaw bone called thecodont. The human being forms two sets of teeth during their life temporary milk or deciduous teeth replaced by a set of permanent or adult teeth called diphyodont. An adult human has 32 permanent teeth which are of four different types called heterodont.

questions.question:
Complete the following reaction:
 Maltose - Maltase $\rightarrow$ Glucose + i
 Lactose - Lactase $\rightarrow$ Glucose + ii
 Sucrose - Sucrase $\rightarrow$ Glucose + iii
questions.question_raw:
Complete the following reaction:

  1. Maltose - Maltase {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Glucose + i
  2. Lactose - Lactase {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Glucose + ii
  3. Sucrose - Sucrase {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} Glucose + iii

options.label: A
options.text:
i - glucose
 ii - galactose
 iii - fructose

correct_option_texts:
i - glucose
 ii - galactose
 iii - fructose
questions.explanation: Maltose in presence of maltase produces two molecules of glucose. Lactose in the presence of lactase produces glucose and galactose. Sucrose in the presence of sucrose produces glucose and fructose.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a part of the colon?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is not a part of the colon?
questions.explanation: The colon is divided into three parts - an ascending, a transverse and a descending part.

questions.question: Brunner’s gland is:
questions.question_raw: Brunner’s gland is:
questions.explanation: Brunner’s glands are submucosal mucin secreting glands localized predominantly in the duodenal bulb and proximal duodenum, decreasing progressively in size and number in the distal portions. Sub-mucosal glands (Brunner’s glands) provide the alkaline medium.

questions.question:
Match the cells and their secretions:
 Cell Type
 Secretion
 a) Goblet cell
 i) succusentericus
 b) Oxyntic cells
 ii) mucus
 c) Chief cells
 iii) acid
 d) Intestinal gland cells
 iv) pepsinogen
questions.question_raw:
Match the cells and their secretions:

Cell Type Secretion
a) Goblet cell i) succusentericus
b) Oxyntic cells ii) mucus
c) Chief cells iii) acid
d) Intestinal gland cells iv) pepsinogen

options.label: A
options.text:
a) - ii
 b) - iii
 c) - i
 d) - iv

options.label: B
options.text:
a) - ii 
 b) - iii 
 c) - iv
 d) - i

options.label: C
options.text:
a) - ii
 b) - i
 c) - iv
 d) - iii

options.label: D
options.text:
a) - iv
 b) - i
 c) - ii
 d) - iii

correct_option_texts:
a) - ii 
 b) - iii 
 c) - iv
 d) - i
questions.explanation: The intestinal mucosal epithelium has goblet cells which secrete mucus. The secretions of the brush border cells of the mucosa along with the secretions of the goblet cells constitute the intestinal juice or succusentericus. The peptic or chief cells which secrete the proenzyme pepsinogen. The parietal or oxyntic cells secrete HCl acid.

questions.question: The part which is common passage for both digestive and respiratory tracts :
questions.question_raw: The part which is common passage for both digestive and respiratory tracts :
questions.explanation: In humans, the pharynx is part of the digestive system and also of the conducting zone of the respiratory system. The oesophagus and the trachea (windpipe) open into the pharynx.

questions.question: All are digestive glands except:
questions.question_raw: All are digestive glands except:
questions.explanation: The thyroid is part of the endocrine system, which is made up of glands that produce, store, and release hormones into the bloodstream so the hormones can reach the body's cells. The thyroid gland uses iodine from the foods you eat to make two main hormones: Triiodothyronine (T_3) Thyroxine (T_4).

questions.question: ________ from pharynx is conveyed into the oesophagus by ________.
questions.question_raw: ________ from pharynx is conveyed into the oesophagus by ________.
questions.explanation: The bolus is conveyed into the pharynx and then into the oesophagus by swallowing or deglutition.

questions.question: The bile duct and the pancreatic duct open together into the duodenum as:
questions.question_raw: The bile duct and the pancreatic duct open together into the duodenum as:
questions.explanation: The bile duct and the pancreatic duct open together into the duodenum as the common hepato-pancreatic duct which is guarded by a sphincter called the sphincter of Oddi.

questions.question: Digestion can be defined as:
questions.question_raw: Digestion can be defined as:

options.label: A
options.text: Intake and direct cellular utilisation of biomacromolecules present in food

options.label: B
options.text: Breakdown of simple molecules into absorbable forms

options.label: C
options.text: Reduction of food molecules to produce energy

options.label: D
options.text: Breakdown of complex molecules into absorbable forms

correct_option_texts: Breakdown of complex molecules into absorbable forms
questions.explanation: Digestion is the breakdown of large insoluble food molecules into small water-soluble food molecules so that they can be absorbed into the watery blood plasma. In certain organisms, these smaller substances are absorbed through the small intestine into the bloodstream.

questions.question: Which among the following is not in the wall of the alimentary canal?
questions.question_raw: Which among the following is not in the wall of the alimentary canal?
questions.explanation: The wall of the alimentary canal from the oesophagus to rectum possesses four layers namely serosa, muscularis, sub-mucosa and mucosa.

questions.question: Which layer of alimentary canal forms villi?
questions.question_raw: Which layer of alimentary canal forms villi?
questions.explanation: Mucosa layer forms irregular folds (rugae) in the stomach and small finger-like foldings called villi in the small intestine.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:
 Opening of the hepato-pancreatic duct into jejunum is guarded by Sphincter of Oddi.
 Duodenum receives bile-pancreatic duct.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following statements:

  1. Opening of the hepato-pancreatic duct into jejunum is guarded by Sphincter of Oddi.
  2. Duodenum receives bile-pancreatic duct.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement B is wrong and A is correct

options.label: B
options.text: Statement B is correct and A is wrong

correct_option_texts: Statement B is correct and A is wrong
questions.explanation: Duodenum receives bile from the pancreatic duct. Bile contains bile juice that contains bile salt used for emulsification of fat. Sphincter of Oddi is not present between hepatic-pancreatic duct into the jejunum.

questions.question: The dental formula for human deciduous and permanent set are 212/212 and 2123/2123. So the deciduous set lacks:
questions.question_raw: The dental formula for human deciduous and permanent set are 212/212 and 2123/2123. So the deciduous set lacks:
questions.explanation: Arrangement of teeth in each half of the upper and lower jaw in the order I, C, P, M, is represented by a dental formula which in human is 2123/2123. 212/212 lacks premolar.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 Nutrient
 Absorbable Forms
 (i) Carbohydrate
 (a) Amino acids
 (ii) Nucleic acid
 (b) Monosaccharides
 (iii) Proteins
 (c) Sugar and bases
 (iv) Lipids
 (d) Fatty acids and glycerol
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

Nutrient Absorbable Forms
(i) Carbohydrate (a) Amino acids
(ii) Nucleic acid (b) Monosaccharides
(iii) Proteins (c) Sugar and bases
(iv) Lipids (d) Fatty acids and glycerol
questions.explanation: Carbohydrate is converted into monosaccharides, the nucleic acid is converted into sugar and bases by nucleosidases, proteins are converted into amino acids by dipeptidases and lipids are converted into fatty acids and glycerol by lipases.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Digestion and Absorption (FA ONLY)

questions.question: Which layer of the alimentary canal is formed of loose connective tissues?
questions.question_raw: Which layer of the alimentary canal is formed of loose connective tissues?
questions.explanation: The submucosa consists of a dense and irregular layer of loose connective tissue with blood vessels, lymphatics, and nerves branching into the mucosa and muscular layer. It contains the submucous plexus, an enteric nervous plexus, situated on the inner surface of the muscular layer.

questions.question: The enzyme which hydrolyzes the starch is_______.
questions.question_raw: The enzyme which hydrolyzes the starch is_______.
questions.explanation: The enzymes that break down or hydrolyze starch into the constituent sugars are known as amylases. Alpha-amylases are found in plants and in animals. Human saliva is rich in amylase, and the pancreas also secretes the enzyme.

questions.question:
What should be filled in to replace the question mark in the following sequence:
?$\to$Trypsinogen $\to$Trypsin
questions.question_raw:
What should be filled in to replace the question mark in the following sequence:
?{tex}\to{/tex}Trypsinogen {tex}\to{/tex}Trypsin
questions.explanation: Trypsinogen is activated by an enzyme, enterokinase, secreted by the intestinal mucosa into active trypsin.

questions.question: The arrows represent :[upward to downward]
questions.question_raw: The arrows represent :[upward to downward]

options.label: C
options.text: Gall bladder and common bile duct

questions.explanation: The figure shows the muscular sac called the gall bladder. The duct of gall bladder (cystic duct) along with the hepatic duct from the liver forms the common bile duct.

questions.question: The secretions released into small intestine are:
questions.question_raw: The secretions released into small intestine are:
questions.explanation: The bile, pancreatic juice, and intestinal juice are the secretions released into the small intestine.

questions.question: Which layer of alimentary canal forms crypts in between the base of villi?
questions.question_raw: Which layer of alimentary canal forms crypts in between the base of villi?
questions.explanation: Mucosa forms gastric glands in the stomach and crypts in between the bases of villi in the intestine (crypts of Lieberkuhn).

questions.question: The optimum pH meant for hydrolyzing starch is______.
questions.question_raw: The optimum pH meant for hydrolyzing starch is______.
questions.explanation: Amylase is an enzyme that catalyses the hydrolysis of starch into sugars. Amylase is present in the saliva of humans and some other mammals, where it begins the chemical process of digestion. it is a major digestive enzyme, and its optimum pH is 6.8

questions.question: An active form of which enzyme hydrolyses milk protein in the stomach?
questions.question_raw: An active form of which enzyme hydrolyses milk protein in the stomach?
questions.explanation: Chymosin, known also as rennin, is a proteolytic enzyme related to pepsin that synthesized by chief cells in the stomach of some animals. Its role in digestion is to curdle or coagulate milk in the stomach, a process of considerable importance in the very young animal.

questions.question: The correct sequence of layers in the gut wall from inside to outside is :
questions.question_raw: The correct sequence of layers in the gut wall from inside to outside is :
questions.explanation: Serosa is the outermost layer while mucosa is the innermost layer. The layer next to mucosa is the sub-mucosa containing nerves, blood, and lymph vessels. Muscularisis found between sub-mucosa and serosa.

questions.question: One of the gut hormones which is often referred to as the hunger hormone since it works as a strong stimulant for appetite and feeding is:
questions.question_raw: One of the gut hormones which is often referred to as the hunger hormone since it works as a strong stimulant for appetite and feeding is:
questions.explanation: Ghrelin the hunger hormone is a peptide hormone produced by ghrelinergic cells in the gastrointestinal tract which function as a neuropeptide in the central nervous system. It stimulates appetite and feeding.

questions.question: Numerous folds present on the inner surface of the stomach are called rugae. Another part of the digestive tract which contains rugae is______.
questions.question_raw: Numerous folds present on the inner surface of the stomach are called rugae. Another part of the digestive tract which contains rugae is______.
questions.explanation: Numerous folds present on the inner surface of the stomach are called rugae. They are a series of ridges produced by the folding of the wall of an organ. Another part of the digestive tract which contains rugae is the palate, the top part of the inside mouth.

questions.question: Which of the following is not secreted from its source in the form of a Zymogen?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is not secreted from its source in the form of a Zymogen?
questions.explanation: Zymogen is an inactive enzyme precursor that requires a biochemical change to become active. The amylase enzyme is not released from its source in the form of a proenzyme or zymogen.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a part of the human alimentary canal?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is not a part of the human alimentary canal?
questions.explanation: The Ilium is the uppermost and largest bone of the pelvis invertebrates. Duodenum jejunum and colon are parts of the human alimentary canal but ileum is not a part of this system.

questions.question: The correct sequence of lipid digestion in alimentary canal is :
questions.question_raw: The correct sequence of lipid digestion in alimentary canal is :
questions.explanation: Digestion of (lipid) fat begins in small intestine by secretion of bile juice that contains bile salts. Bile salt emulsify the lipids into globules that is further simplified into micelles and chylomicrons.

questions.question: In elephant and walrus, projecting tusks are:
questions.question_raw: In elephant and walrus, projecting tusks are:
questions.explanation: The projecting tusk of elephant and walrus are incisors and canines respectively. These teeth are not used for chewing food.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Digestion and Absorption (FA ONLY)

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is/ are incorrect for the Enteric nervous system:
 neurons of Myentric plexus regulates glandular secretions in the GI tract.
 neurons of Meissner’s plexus regulates peristaltic waves.
questions.question_raw:
Which of the following statement is/ are incorrect for the Enteric nervous system:

  1. neurons of Myentric plexus regulates glandular secretions in the GI tract.
  2. neurons of Meissner’s plexus regulates peristaltic waves.

options.label: C
options.text: ii is correct and i is incorrect

options.label: D
options.text: i is correct and ii is incorrect

questions.explanation:
The inner plexus is the main control for GI secretion and local blood flow. The myenteric plexus is principally responsible for the peristaltic movement of the bowels. It extends throughout the entire length of the gut as a linear chain of interconnected neurons.
The Meissner or submucosal plexus regulates the configuration of the luminal surface, controls glandular secretions, alters electrolyte and water transport, and regulates local blood flow.

questions.question: Every cell contains DNA and when we consume plant/animal material, the DNA which reaches our digestive tract is digested by:
questions.question_raw: Every cell contains DNA and when we consume plant/animal material, the DNA which reaches our digestive tract is digested by:

options.label: A
options.text: Enzymes present in bile juice and gastric juice

options.label: B
options.text: Enzymes present in bile juice and succusentericus

options.label: C
options.text: Enzymes present in pancreatic juice and succus entericus

options.label: D
options.text: Enzymes present in pancreatic juice and gastric juice

correct_option_texts: Enzymes present in pancreatic juice and succus entericus
questions.explanation: Food material consists of plant or animal cells. Each cell contains DNA in the nucleus. Digestion of DNA takes place in the small intestine by the action of enzyme succus entricus.

questions.question: The process by which small electrolytes are absorbed:
questions.question_raw: The process by which small electrolytes are absorbed:
questions.explanation: Small amounts of monosaccharides like glucose, amino acids, and some of the electrolytes like chloride ions are generally absorbed by simple diffusion.

questions.question: That part of alimentary canal which is responsible for absorption of water, simple sugars, and alcohol:
questions.question_raw: That part of alimentary canal which is responsible for absorption of water, simple sugars, and alcohol:
questions.explanation:
Absorption in different parts of the digestive system: 
 The small intestine is the primary site for the absorption of water, simple sugars (like glucose), and alcohol into the bloodstream.
 The stomach absorbs very little, though alcohol and some drugs can be absorbed here.
 The large intestine primarily absorbs water and electrolytes, but not simple sugars or alcohol.
 The mouth is involved in digestion but does not play a significant role in absorption.

questions.question: Select which one is correctly matched pair:
questions.question_raw: Select which one is correctly matched pair:

options.label: A
options.text: Glucose - absorbed by passive transport that doesn’t need energy

options.label: B
options.text: Amino acids - transported by simple diffusion that doesn’t need energy

options.label: C
options.text: Fructose - transported by facilitated diffusion that doesn’t need energy

options.label: D
options.text: Galactose - absorbed by active transport that doesn’t need energy

correct_option_texts: Fructose - transported by facilitated diffusion that doesn’t need energy
questions.explanation: Some of the substances like fructose and some amino acids are absorbed with the help of the carrier ions like sodium ions. This mechanism is called facilitated transport.

questions.question: Absorption of food takes place in stomach as well as intestine but villi are present in intestine only because:
questions.question_raw: Absorption of food takes place in stomach as well as intestine but villi are present in intestine only because:

options.label: A
options.text: The stomach is already provided with microvilli

options.label: B
options.text: Intestine receives better blood supply than other parts of the alimentary canal

options.label: C
options.text: Food is converted into absorbable form within the intestine

options.label: D
options.text: The stomach is not much provided with microvilli

correct_option_texts: Food is converted into absorbable form within the intestine
questions.explanation: Villi increase the surface area thereby increasing the absorption of food. The villi also contain lymph vessels for absorbing the products of fat-digestion. From the bloodstream, the absorbed food is finally delivered to each and every cell of the body. The mucosal walls of the stomach form irregular fold known as rugae. These help in increasing the surface area to volume ratio of the expanding stomach.

questions.question: Absorption of fats takes place through:
questions.question_raw: Absorption of fats takes place through:
questions.explanation: Lymph is the extracellular fluid that constitutes the lymphatic system in the human body. The absorption of fats takes place through lymphatic ducts. As fats cannot be carried through narrow capillaries.

questions.question:
Trans fats:
 they are found in most processed and fast foods for eg. French fries.
 they help food stay fresh for a long time.
 they are a much better type of fat than saturated fats.
questions.question_raw:
Trans fats:

  1. they are found in most processed and fast foods for eg. French fries.
  2. they help food stay fresh for a long time.
  3. they are a much better type of fat than saturated fats.

options.label: B
options.text: Only i and iii are correct

options.label: C
options.text: Only i is the correct statement

options.label: D
options.text: Only i and ii are correct

correct_option_texts: Only i and ii are correct
questions.explanation: Trans fats are hydrogenated fats which make the oil more solid. They are found in most processed and fast foods. They help food stay fresh for a long time.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:
 In ruminants, cellulose digesting bacteria are present in the digestive system.
 Such digestion by microbial activity is called Anaerobic digestion.
 Camels are ruminants lacking the omasum chamber.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following statements:

  1. In ruminants, cellulose digesting bacteria are present in the digestive system.
  2. Such digestion by microbial activity is called Anaerobic digestion.
  3. Camels are ruminants lacking the omasum chamber.
questions.explanation:
In ruminants like cows, buffalos, cellulose digesting bacteria are present in the digestive system that release cellulose enzymes.
 Anaerobic digestion is a collection of processes by which microorganisms break down biodegradable material in the absence of oxygen.
 Camel is a pseudo ruminant as it has only 3 chambers instead of 4.

questions.question: If an imaginary food contains 5 grams of protein, 4 grams of fat and 10 grams of carbohydrates. Now calculate total calories it would provide ( given 1g protein = 4 calories, 1g fat = 10 calories, 1g carbohydrates = 4 calories):
questions.question_raw: If an imaginary food contains 5 grams of protein, 4 grams of fat and 10 grams of carbohydrates. Now calculate total calories it would provide ( given 1g protein = 4 calories, 1g fat = 10 calories, 1g carbohydrates = 4 calories):
questions.explanation: Five gram of protein will produce 5 $\times $ 4 = 20 calories, 4 g of fat = 4 $\times $ 10 = 40 calories and 10 g of carbohydrates = 10 $\times $ 4 = 40 calories. So total energy produced is equal to 20 + 40 + 40 = 100 calories.

questions.question: Correct sequence of layers in gut wall from inside to outside is:
questions.question_raw: Correct sequence of layers in gut wall from inside to outside is:
questions.explanation: The wall of the alimentary canal is made of four layers performing different functions. The innermost layer is called mucosa followed by submucosa,muscularis and serosa. These layers are made of different kind of tissues to perform a different function.

questions.question: The reflex action of vomiting is controlled by ______.
questions.question_raw: The reflex action of vomiting is controlled by ______.
questions.explanation: Vomiting is believed to be controlled by two distinct brain centres - the vomiting centre and the chemoreceptor trigger zone - both located in the medulla oblongata. The vomiting centre initiates and controls the act of emesis, which involves a series of contractions of the smooth muscles lining the digestive tract.

questions.question: Which digestive disorder reduces the absorption of food?
questions.question_raw: Which digestive disorder reduces the absorption of food?
questions.explanation: The abnormal frequency of bowel movement and increased liquidity of the fecal discharge reduces the absorption of food leading to diarrhoea.

questions.question: Which one is a mismatched pair?
questions.question_raw: Which one is a mismatched pair?
questions.explanation: The abnormal frequency of bowel movement and increased liquidity of the faecal discharge is known as diarrhoea.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Digestion and Absorption (FA ONLY)

questions.question:
Match the following:
 Digestive Disorder
 Cause
 (a) Constipation
 (i) gallstones
 (b) Jaundice
 (ii) reflex action anxiety
 (c) Indigestion
 (iii) intentional ischemia
 (d) Vomiting
 (iv) irregular bowel movements
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

Digestive Disorder Cause
(a) Constipation (i) gallstones
(b) Jaundice (ii) reflex action anxiety
(c) Indigestion (iii) intentional ischemia
(d) Vomiting (iv) irregular bowel movements
questions.explanation: The causes of indigestion are inadequate enzyme secretion, anxiety, food poisoning, overeating, and spicy food. In constipation, the bowel movements occur irregularly. In jaundice, the deposition of bile pigments results in gall stones. A reflex action is controlled by the vomiting centre in the medulla.

questions.question: Malnutrition covers:
questions.question_raw: Malnutrition covers:

options.label: B
options.text: All the diseases which infants suffer from

questions.explanation: Malnutrition is the condition of having less than the required amount of food or more than the required amount of food. Both conditions are harmful to health. So, malnutrition covers both undernutrition and overnutrition.

questions.question: Bacteria responsible for chronic gastritis is:
questions.question_raw: Bacteria responsible for chronic gastritis is:
questions.explanation: Chronic gastritis involves swelling or inflammation of the stomach lining. Helicobacter pylori colonize the stomach and induce chronic gastritis, a long-lasting inflammation of the stomach.

questions.question: Regurgitation is more common in infants than in adults. The reason will be:
questions.question_raw: Regurgitation is more common in infants than in adults. The reason will be:

options.label: A
options.text: Pyloric sphincter is much less effective in infants

options.label: B
options.text: Excessive acid formation and secretion in the stomach of infants

options.label: C
options.text: Cardiac sphincter is much less effective in infants

options.label: D
options.text: Peristaltic waves are not strong enough in infants

correct_option_texts: Cardiac sphincter is much less effective in infants
questions.explanation: Regurgitation is the expulsion of material from the pharynx, or esophagus, usually characterized by the presence of undigested food or blood. Regurgitation is used by a number of species to feed their young. Regurgitation is more common in infants than adults because the cardiac sphincter is much less effective in infants.

questions.question: ________ from pharynx is conveyed into oesophagus by ________.
questions.question_raw: ________ from pharynx is conveyed into oesophagus by ________.
questions.explanation: Bolus from pharynx is passed into the oesophagus by deglutition. The bolus is the semisolid chewed food mixed with saliva in the mouth. The food from pharynx passed to the oesophagus by swallowing.

questions.question:
Read the following statements about CCK:
 This hormone stimulates gastrointestinal motility and pancreatic endocrine function.
 This hormone stimulates release of bile from the gall bladder.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following statements about CCK:

  1. This hormone stimulates gastrointestinal motility and pancreatic endocrine function.
  2. This hormone stimulates release of bile from the gall bladder.

options.label: B
options.text: Statement ii is correct and i is wrong

options.label: C
options.text: Statement ii is wrong and i is correct

correct_option_texts: Statement ii is correct and i is wrong
questions.explanation:
Cholecystokinin, previously called pancreozymins, is synthesized and secreted by enteroendocrine cells in the duodenum, the first segment of the small intestine.
Its presence causes the release of digestive enzymes and bile from the pancreas and gallbladder, respectively, and also acts as a hunger suppressant.

questions.question:
People who are lactose intolerant:
 Cannot digest food with lactose in them.
 They need to take a calcium supplement.
questions.question_raw:
People who are lactose intolerant:

  1. Cannot digest food with lactose in them.
  2. They need to take a calcium supplement.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement ii is correct and i is wrong

options.label: B
options.text: Statement ii is wrong and i is correct

questions.explanation: People who are lactose intolerant cannot digest food with lactose in them and these people need to take a calcium supplement which is naturally present along with lactose in milk.

questions.question: Liver stores extra glycogen but once storage limit is reached then extra carbohydrate will be:
questions.question_raw: Liver stores extra glycogen but once storage limit is reached then extra carbohydrate will be:
questions.explanation: Glycogen is the form in which the body stores glucose. The liver can only store about 100 g of glucose in the form of glycogen. The muscles also store glycogen. Muscles can store approximately 500 g of glycogen. Because of the limited storage areas, any carbohydrates that are consumed beyond the storage capacity are converted to and stored as fat. There is practically no limit on how many calories the body can store as fat.

questions.question: In Reema’s body, substances in bile harden forming stone in the gall bladder. What can be the possible treatment?
questions.question_raw: In Reema’s body, substances in bile harden forming stone in the gall bladder. What can be the possible treatment?

options.label: B
options.text: Removing only stone from gall bladder by surgery

options.label: D
options.text: Dissolving the stone with medicinal treatment

questions.explanation: The substance in bile hardens to form stone in the gall bladder. It is difficult to remove stone surgically and by lithotripsy. So, in most cases, the gall bladder is removed surgically.

questions.question: Which of the following is a mismatched pair?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is a mismatched pair?
questions.explanation: Diastema is the gap between teeth. Generally, a diastema is not present in human beings. Rabbit has rootless teeth and frogs are acrodont as same teeth remain throughout their life.

questions.question:
Blood vessels of the alimentary canal:
 provide nourishment to the cells of the alimentary canal.
 absorb digested fat molecules from the stomach and intestine.
questions.question_raw:
Blood vessels of the alimentary canal:

  1. provide nourishment to the cells of the alimentary canal.
  2. absorb digested fat molecules from the stomach and intestine.

options.label: B
options.text: Statement i is wrong and ii is correct

options.label: C
options.text: Statement i is correct and ii is wrong

correct_option_texts: Statement i is correct and ii is wrong
questions.explanation: Blood vessels of the alimentary canal provide nourishment to the cells of the alimentary canal but do not absorb digested fat molecules from the stomach and intestine.

questions.question: Out of the following which one doesn’t provide any calories?
questions.question_raw: Out of the following which one doesn’t provide any calories?
questions.explanation: Fats, protein, and alcohol on oxidation produce energy along with other products but vitamins are not oxidized to release energy to us. They act as protective food to protect us from the disease.

questions.question:
Match the cells and their secretions:
 Cell Type
 Secretion
 (a) Goblet cell
 (i) succus entericus
 (b) Oxyntic cells
 (ii) mucus
 (c) Chief cells
 (iii) acid
 (d) Intestinal gland cells
 (iv) pepsinogen
questions.question_raw:
Match the cells and their secretions:

Cell Type Secretion
(a) Goblet cell (i) succus entericus
(b) Oxyntic cells (ii) mucus
(c) Chief cells (iii) acid
(d) Intestinal gland cells (iv) pepsinogen
questions.explanation: Mucus is secreted by Goblet cell, pepsinogen is released from Chief cells, Hydrochloric acid is released by Oxyntic cells and succus entericus enzymes are released from intestinal gland cells.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 GI hormone
 Function
 (a) Enterocrinin
 (i) stimulates gastric juice secretion
 (b) Duocrinin
 (ii) inhibits gastric juice secretion
 (c) Gastrin
 (iii) stimulates secretory activity of brunner’s glands
 (d) Enterogasterone
 (iv) stimulates secretory activity of crypts of lieberkuhn
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

GI hormone Function
(a) Enterocrinin (i) stimulates gastric juice secretion
(b) Duocrinin (ii) inhibits gastric juice secretion
(c) Gastrin (iii) stimulates secretory activity of brunner’s glands
(d) Enterogasterone (iv) stimulates secretory activity of crypts of lieberkuhn
questions.explanation: Gastrointestinal hormone Enterocrinin stimulates secretory activity of crypts of Lieberkuhn. Duocrinin stimulates the secretory activity of Brunner’s glands. Gastrin stimulates gastric juice secretion. Enterogasterone inhibits gastric juice secretion.

questions.question: Parodontax toothpaste launched a few months ago that helps to prevent periodontal disease which are caused by microbe named:
questions.question_raw: Parodontax toothpaste launched a few months ago that helps to prevent periodontal disease which are caused by microbe named:
questions.explanation: Porphyromonas gingivalis is a Gram-negative oral anaerobe that is involved in the pathogenesis of periodontitis, an inflammatory disease that destroys the tissues supporting the tooth which eventually may lead to tooth loss. Paodontax toothpaste help to prevent periodontal disease.
